purpose: List and upsert Funds (the second tier in CCH fund accounting - the individual fund records under a Fund Type).

delete.notes: No eligibility pre-flight - assigned accounts cascade to Unassigned.

id_field_naming_gotcha: Body field `engagementId` carries the clientId, not the engagementId. Same misnaming as the rest of workbench-api.

funds.id: int (0 for new entries - Fund follows the id=0-means-new convention)
funds.name: string
funds.fundSubTypeId: int? (optional - the FundSubType.id if the fund belongs to a sub-type)

response_shape_get.field_name_asymmetry: GET returns prefixed names and embeds fundType + fundSubType as full objects; PUT expects bare names with fundTypeId / fundSubTypeId as integer FKs. Use scripts.funds.normalize_funds() to convert.

semantics.put_is_whole_list_replace: Same as FundType - PUT replaces the entire funds collection. Caller MUST include every existing fund (with id) plus new ones (id=0).
semantics.ui_quirk: Unlike FundType, the Funds tab UI does batch multiple Adds into one PUT on Save. Programmatically you can do the same.
semantics.fund_sub_type_assignment: Assigning a fund to a sub-type happens through this same endpoint by setting fundSubTypeId. The UI also exposes it on the Fund Sub-Type tab as a save-after-edit workflow that ultimately fires this PUT.

verification: After upsert, GET /v1/Fund/{clientId} and confirm the new row exists with a server-assigned id and the expected fundTypeId / fundSubTypeId.
